qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the CCDB portal?
qa_pairs.answer: The CCDB is the portal to your Alliance account, providing information on your project roles, allocated resources, and usage statistics.

qa_pairs.question: What tasks can I perform on the CCDB portal?
qa_pairs.answer: On the CCDB portal, you can register, manage personal information and roles, apply for the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC), and manage RAP information and membership.

qa_pairs.question: What is a CCI and how is it created?
qa_pairs.answer: A CCI is a unique personal and national identifier, created when you register at https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca, and is formatted as three letters followed by three digits (e.g., abc-123).

qa_pairs.question: Who is eligible to register for a CCI with the CCDB?
qa_pairs.answer: To register, you must either be a faculty member at a CCDB-registered Canadian institution or be sponsored by one. Sponsored individuals can include graduate students and research staff who report to the sponsoring faculty member.

qa_pairs.question: What is a role in the context of CCDB, and why is it important?
qa_pairs.answer: A role is an identifier representing a person's position, institution, and sponsor. A valid and up-to-date role is necessary to access computational resources, and new roles are required for changes to maintain accurate records for funding agencies.

qa_pairs.question: What is a CCRI and how is it formatted?
qa_pairs.answer: A CCRI is the unique identifier for your role, formatted as your CCI followed by two digits (e.g., abc-123-01). Since a person can have multiple roles over time, one CCI may be associated with more than one CCRI.

qa_pairs.question: What is a Resource Allocation Project (RAP)?
qa_pairs.answer: A RAP is a group of researchers to whom national computational resources are allocated, identified by a RAPI and an associated group name, used for reporting usage statistics.

qa_pairs.question: What are the two main types of RAPs?
qa_pairs.answer: The two main types of RAPs are Default RAPs, automatically created when a PI role is activated, and RAC RAPs, created when a PI receives an award through the RAC application process.

qa_pairs.question: What is a group name in the context of RAPs?
qa_pairs.answer: A group name is an alias for the Research Allocation Project Identifier (RAPI), providing an easier-to-remember unique identifier for each RAP, and is used as a POSIX group name.

qa_pairs.question: Who has access to a PI's Default RAP?
qa_pairs.answer: All of a PI's activated sponsored user roles are automatically members of the PI's Default RAP, which cannot be modified, though a PI can deactivate any sponsored role.

qa_pairs.question: How does membership work for RAC RAPs with HPC resources?
qa_pairs.answer: For RAC RAPs with HPC resources, all of the PI's sponsored user roles, associated Co-PI roles, and sponsored user roles of Co-PIs are automatically added as members when the RAP is created. Any new role sponsored by the PI later is also added.

qa_pairs.question: How does membership work for RAC RAPs with Cloud resources?
qa_pairs.answer: For RAC RAPs with Cloud resources, only the PI is added as a member when the RAP is created.

qa_pairs.question: Can a PI modify the membership of a RAC RAP?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, a PI can modify the membership of any of their RAC RAPs at any time, adding any user with an active Alliance account or removing sponsored user roles.

qa_pairs.question: What are the three permission levels for RAP membership?
qa_pairs.answer: The three RAP membership permission levels are Owner (solely the PI), Manager (delegated by the owner or another manager to make membership changes and use allocations), and Member (can use allocations but not modify membership).

qa_pairs.question: If I had an Alliance account previously but my position or sponsor changed, or I forgot my password, should I create a new account?
qa_pairs.answer: No, each person is allowed only one Alliance account (one CCI). If your position or sponsor changed, you should apply for a new role using your existing account. If you forgot your password, you can reset it, or email accounts@tech.alliancecan.ca if you can't access your email on file.

qa_pairs.question: How do I sign up for an Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: To sign up, go to the register link on the CCDB portal, where you will need to accept certain policies and agreements found on the Alliance website.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I review the agreements I've accepted on CCDB?
qa_pairs.answer: After your account is approved, you can review accepted agreements at any time on CCDB by navigating to 'My Account --> Agreements'.

qa_pairs.question: What are the main types of roles when applying for an account?
qa_pairs.answer: The two main types are 'sponsor' roles, primarily for faculty members (Primary Investigators or PIs) from eligible Canadian post-secondary academic institutions, and 'sponsored' roles.

qa_pairs.question: What types of sponsored roles are available?
qa_pairs.answer: Sponsored roles include students (undergraduate, masters, doctoral), researchers affiliated with the PI's institution (postdoctoral fellow, researcher), non-research staff, and external collaborators from different institutions.

qa_pairs.question: What is the process after I submit my account request?
qa_pairs.answer: After submission, you will receive an email to confirm your address. If you are a PI, your application is approved by Federation staff; otherwise, your identified sponsor must confirm it before Federation staff approval.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact for help with questions not covered in the FAQ?
qa_pairs.answer: For any questions not covered in the FAQ, you should send an email to accounts@tech.alliancecan.ca.
